LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WAHIB TELDJA, C&J LEASING, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS ROYAL TAXI'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI AND HER COUNSEL. CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND DEFENDANT WAHIB BOU TELDJA'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED IS DENIED. (CODE CIV. PROC. 2033.280(C); ST. MARY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2014) 223 CAL.APP.4TH 762, 776 ["A RESPONDING PARTY'S SERVICE, PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE 'DEEMED ADMITTED' MOTION, OF SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIANT RESPONSES, WILL DEFEAT A PROPOUNDING PARTY'S ATTEMPT UNDER SECTION 2033.280 TO HAVE THE RFAS DEEMED ADMITTED."].) THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH IT IS NORMALLY MANDATORY THAT THE COURT IMPOSE A MONETARY SANCTION ON THE PARTY OR ATTORNEY, OR BOTH, WHOSE FAILURE TO SERVE A TIMELY RESPONSE TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NECESSITATED A MOTION TO COMPEL (ID.), THE COURT DECLINES TO DO SO UNDER THE UNUSUAL CIRCUMSTANCES PRESENTED HERE. DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI'S COUNSEL DECLARES HE NEVER RECEIVED THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS. (PURTELL DECL. 2.) FURTHER, TELDJA FILED HIS MOTION WITHOUT FIRST MEETING AND CONFERRING WITH OPPOSING COUNSEL, AND NOWINSKI SERVED VERIFIED RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS WITHIN THREE DAYS AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED, WHICH SUGGESTS THAT THE NEED TO FILE THE MOTION COULD HAVE BEEN AVOIDED ENTIRELY, HAD COUNSEL COMMUNICATED IN ADVANCE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Cross-Complainant Defendant Amanda Nowinski And Her Counsel
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas W. Purtell, Esq., Law Offices of Thomas W. J. Purtell, Telephone 415-722-6291, appearing for Defendant(s) Amanda Nowinski, et al; (CourtCall)
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WAHIB TELDJA, C&J LEASING, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS ROYAL TAXI's Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Cross-Complainant Defendant Amanda Nowinski And Her Counsel.

Cross-Complainant and Defendant Wahib Bou Teldja's motion to have requests for admission deemed admitted is denied. (Code Civ. Proc. 2033.280(c); St. Mary v. Superior Court (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 762, 776 ["a responding party's service, prior to the hearing on the 'deemed admitted' motion, of substantially compliant responses, will defeat a propounding party's attempt under section 2033.280 to have the RFAs deemed admitted."].) The request for sanctions is denied. Although it is normally mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated a motion to compel (id.), the Court declines to do so under the unusual circumstances presented here. Defendant Amanda Nowinski's counsel declares he never received the requests for admissions. (Purtell Decl. 2.) Further, Teldja filed his motion without first meeting and conferring with opposing counsel, and Nowinski served verified responses to the requests for admissions within three days after the motion was filed, which suggests that the need to file the motion could have been avoided entirely, had counsel communicated in advance.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Defendant, Cross-Defendant, and Cross-Complainant Nowinski's motion for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice. Nowinski proposes to settle this personal injury case for $1,500. For the Court to find that a settlement is in good faith, the settlors must provide substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of their liability. (See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6; Toyota Motor Sales USA, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 864, 871.) In addition, the court needs to roughly approximate the plaintiff's total recovery to determine whether a settlement is in good faith. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

In this case, Nowinski presents no evidence that allows the Court to approximate plaintiff's total recovery. There is no evidence regarding plaintiff's injuries, medical bills, lost wages, etc. Plaintiff's $60,000 settlement with co-defendants suggests that the $1,500 proposed settlement may not be in the ballpark, especially since the record demonstrates that Nowinski opened the cab door into plaintiff without looking. (Nowinski Depo., 23:4-6.) This undisputed evidence does not support Nowinski's assertion that her proportionate liability is 0%.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Defendant, Cross-Defendant, and Cross-Complainant Amanda Nowinski's motion for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice to its renewal upon a proper evidentiary showing. Defendant has not presented any admissible evidence that would enable the Court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiff['s] total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v., Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.' " (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].) Counsel's declaration, which lacks personal knowledge or a proper foundation, does not constitute such substantial evidence. (Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834 [attorney's declaration regarding settling defendant's liability insufficient where he failed to provide specific supporting facts or expert opinion].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not reported. =(302/EPS)
